item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Probably my favorite SCP of all time, left me crying for a long time afterwards.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Did I seriously cry over a freaking box?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is absolutely beautiful. This skip made it into the Containment Breach game, and I loved seeing the video of the dragons pouring out of the box. This is a wonderful entry.

item.title: You made my heart cry
item.content:encoded: This was a lovely story from start to finish. I've always loved fantasy adventure worlds and this really was a sucker punch to the feels - wonderful work.

item.title: Oh god why do people give up on fantasy
item.content:encoded: Ok I know that dragons and stuff don't seem real but I still believe in them. After all, there are stories of dragons all over the world and from different civilizations. Giants are also quite popular in mythology, like the jotuns from the Vikings and Goliath from the Bible to the Titans in Greece and Rome. Fairies/sprites/whatever you want to call them are everywhere in the old stories. That's just 3 examples, there's a whole lot more of evidence that the mythological creatures might have existed, since the myths about them come from all over the world.

item.title: Thank-you for making this
item.content:encoded: I went through making this profile for the site just so I could say I love this scp and wish there was more information about it, I wish there was more to it outside of what others had imagined for it, though I guess this whole scp is about imagination.

item.title: Would Love to Add to this!
item.content:encoded: I'm currently wanting to make a webtoon based on trying to save this world and bring the dragons back. Not to Earth, unless Oz wanted to, but I wanted to make a story restoring the Fantastic lands to their former glory. Sent Oz a message, I hope he sees!! It doesn't have to be directly tied to the SCP itself, but the lore and the world… its amazing and I want to use it to write a beautiful story of prophecy, magic and restoration of a truly amazing world Oz created.

item.title: Re: Description Tense
item.content:encoded: Out of universe, I'd imagine that including that line in the description would most certainly dampen the blow of the anomaly's neutralization at the end. In universe, it wouldn't be too out of place to archive a description, even if the anomalous effects still don't exist anymore.

item.title: Description Tense
item.content:encoded:
Since the box’s label changed, should the description read:

“The words "HERE BE DRAGONS" were handwritten in black permanent marker on the lid of the container, though these works have since changed to “HERE WERE DRAGONS” as of the end of the Jabberwocky Event.”

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not required, but it's considered a basic courtesy to let the author know you intend to write something involving a page they wrote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Correct me if I'm wrong, but I believe author permission is not required to write tales.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hey, is OZ Ouroborous still around? I'm interested in writing a Tale for this wonderful SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why am I crying over paper dragons. Take my god damn upvote

item.title: Something Interesting
item.content:encoded: I just realized something interesting, this SCP is very reminiscent of the How to Train Your Dragon series (the books). To be specific, it reminds me of the ending line of the last book How to Fight a Dragon's Fury, "Books are like Dragons, if we stop believing in them, then they will cease to exist." (or something along those lines).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Instant favorite. I would like to read more SCP like this one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
sad this had to be changed, but the way it tells a story of war while being all inside a box, it's amazing
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: please… I can only cry so much. just take this and let me cry in peace. *gives +1*

item.title: A Sequel is dearly needed...
item.content:encoded:
If there was ever a time for dragons to return, here be now. It’s a interesting idea, and I love that there was a time in which we believed in dragons so we connected to another world full of fantasy. But as time progressed, and man modernized, the effect became disorienting to both men of science (or in the Foundation’s case, partially science) and the creatures of fantasy.

It’s sad that it all ends in tears and leaving the land of fantasy, but I do hope that if these creatures and men are able, will find a way to come here. Maybe something that helps rekindles the bridge between modern reality, and whimsical fantasy. Rarely does the Foundation connect to that (as it’s always feels like it’s full of horrors, and not even normal horrors), but I really do hope to see more of the world of fantasy reach out to the world of science to make something fantastical.

+1 and a recommendation to bring more elements of your fantasy to Foundation reality.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
how dare you make me sad like this. illegal.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wanna know about the new fantasy…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Disclaimer: I don't know if the author deleted it for a reason (i.e., that they specifically wished that it no longer be available to readers) — if so, could someone please let me know?

With that said, an archived version of Dragon's Fall can be found here:
https://web.archive.org/web/20180615033136/http://scp-wiki.wikidot.com/dragon-s-fall

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Just like many others before me, this definitely hit me in the feels hard! And also just like the user StartRunning,ーwho's comment you can find on page 4 of this discussion threadーthis is my first ever time making a comment.

I found this SCP while looking for something to watch on YouTube. It was a reading on TheVolgun's channel. The video was made with the help of the developers of the SCP: Containment Breach Unity game. I am very glad I watched and listened. If only the happy ending of that video was here in the official article. Either way, I'm definitely giving this a +1!

I also wonder where Dragon's Fall went. It seems to have been deleted. Was hoping to read it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This and 093 are still my 2 favorites to this day. Rip my baby dragons :(

item.title: It’s enough to make a grown man cry, and that’s ok.
item.content:encoded: This is an amazing example of how to combine an scp and a story into one. This scp is easily one of my favorites. I can’t really list any bad things about this scp. It applies to how the foundation writes its entries, it holds an emotional connection to the reader, and it keeps the reader entertained throughout. This should have more +’s than it already does in my opinion. One last thing: bonus points for adding the book as well, read every last word.
By far one of my favorite articles in the entire site. I just hope one day they will come back somehow…

item.title: Re: One last time
item.content:encoded: He did, but it seems to have been deleted. I think it's on page 4 or 5 of this discussion page.

item.title: Lump in the Throat
item.content:encoded:
I put on the Breath of the Wild theme for the dragons while I read this. It gave it even more of an emotional impact than I thought possible:

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=nBX9RazKuG4

item.title: One last time
item.content:encoded: Im confused as to why the crystal would say this. Also did the author ever write more tales about this? (Minus the book)

item.title: Wow....
item.content:encoded: I swear I almost cried

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This SCP article is one of the best I have ever read on the site. It invokes emotions in the reader that you wouldn't imagine feeling for a box full of sentient arts and crafts. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, I'm depressed now.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My faith in Dragons shall remain strong, no matter what. I believe in those glorious creatures, through and through. While not religious myself, and having a burning hatred for it, I must admit, I do borderline worship them. This SCP is…mixed feelings, though. Sad, yes, but a little…eh.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Upvoted it myself but I agree - it's excellent, and a breath of fresh feels compared to much of the content around here, but the one major niggle I have with it is the lack of specifics you'd expect from a proper containment log. Where was it found? What events led to it being brought into Foundation possession? Detailed logs of testing? More specifications as to how it's contained? It just seems like it sat in a box for a year or so until it disintegrated.

Still, good work, OP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
i'm not crying my eyes are just dry
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've heard about this one for so long, so much hype and finally got the chance to read it!
TBH I wish I hadn't fallen for the hype. It was very interesting and I liked it but I wasn't reduced to tears like everyone else has. I didn't even find it very sad at all really. So yeah entertaining but not heartbreaking.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My god this SCP was traumatizing. The story was really good and every note was close to bringing tears to my heart. I love it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: They should have let a kid play with it.

item.title: Re: Hmm...
item.content:encoded: Yeah, I'm missing the point…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm an emotional person, as much as I hate to accept.
So reading this to the end I honest to God teared up. I always heard people talk about this SCP and how sad it was, but I never took the time to read it. I'm glad I did.

These little dragons tear at your heartstrings. They actually care for us, all the way to the end. You want to help them, but unsure how. So they suffer and die off. It makes the reader feel helpless.

Farewell SCP 1762 you shall be remembered and respected…

item.title: :D
item.content:encoded: I love this so much, it's brilliantly sad and cool at the same time. I got a bunch of Origami paper recently so I'm gonna make a ton of different kinds of Dragons and try and recreate this SCP :D

item.title: Hmm...
item.content:encoded:
I kinda agree with Monte here. Although I think this is well-written, why was the SCP not able/willing to help them? Like, 11 months and 28 days and they didn't try to do anything about it???

EDIT: Wait, I see now. The page states that they tried to send messages back, but it didn't work. I wonder why, though…

EDIT 2: Wait a second… why couldn't they have just spoken directly to the dragons that came out of the box?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I actually can't upvote this. It is good, for sure, but the way the Foundation did nothing at all during all the events or about the letters kinds of watered things down too much.

The Foundation could believe or not believe. It could react with concern, it could experiment with the things, it could just state, as an observation log, intently doing nothing.

But it just seems to not really be there. It is stated that the dragons play with personnel, but that's it. It seems more that the Foundation just found the letters and the charred box.

Well, at least too good for downvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Four years later, this is still one of my favorites. Heck, I even got a little choked up when I saw it in the In The Pines montage.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is where the dragons went.
They lie …
Not dead, not asleep. Not waiting, because waiting implies expectation. Possibly the word we're looking for here is …
… dormant.
And although the space they occupy isn't like normal space, nevertheless they are packed in tightly. Not a cubic inch there but is filled by a claw, a talon, a scale, the tip of a tail, so the effect is like one of those trick drawings and your eyeballs eventually realise that the space between each dragon is, in fact, another dragon:
They could put you in mind of a can of sardines, if you thought sardines were huge and scaly and proud and arrogant.
And presumably, somewhere, there's the key.

—Guards Guards, by Terry Pratchett

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Saw that in Containment. Wondered why people in the comments appeared sad. Now I know.

:,(

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is an excellent, beautiful but impressively sad SCP. The emotions that cause are notables, the lector can empathize with the little dragons and sorry this Fate. Until the end, the little dragons think in “that person”…

After reading it, I have a doubt: For the context of some phrases, I can understand that “the friend” was one person, for example, when SCP-1762-1 asks for his/her family (How has your Family been?), but in some messages it uses the word “friends”, for example in You have found us. Thank you. It has been so long since we last saw each other, friends. It is refered that the employees of the Fundation are their friends, or is a way to emphasis that “that person” and the dragons are friends?. I hope that you can explain me that.

Thanks for this article

Best regards,
Yay for dragons!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The dragons will only comeback if you believe in them. That's the message that was in the letters.
I'm not sure if I'm correct.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Like others have said, this was moving and smacks of nostalgia in a very bittersweet way that words can't explain. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Impressively sad. I feel sad for this little made-up origami world like i haven't in a long time, long time.

+1, i feel ill.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Just beautiful… I am reminded of my own SCP, and many others I have read and enjoyed. It also made me sad, thinking about the dragons all disappearing and the Foundation being unable to help.

But if they could have, would they?

+1'd.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is the title from Discworld?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Nooooooooooooooooooooo

A child's fantasy and a belief in dragons dies before my eyes, and it makes me sad.

So much +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well that was a much bleaker ending then I had hoped, reading the Dragon's Fall story I had hopes that somehow 1762-1 would have reactivated in some way to a reduced extent.

Guess I got my hopes up too much.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: With the recommendation from good user DrBleep, I have finally updated one last farewell to the origami dragons. There is now one last addendum, finally closing the door to Fantasy for good, along with a special book. Such nostalgia, I wrote this over 3 years ago. :')

item.title: Re: +1
item.content:encoded: Thank you so much West! ^^

This is by far my 5-6th favorite skip. That's an accomplishment to do, as I've read like 70% of the series. I love this to bits, and then I love the bits.

+500

item.title: Two Year Anniversary
item.content:encoded: Has it been two years already? Wow, this brings back memories. I really do want to come back and keep up the work on this universe I made, but jeez, college life has so much going on, both academic and social-wise. Hopefully once finals are done I can catch a brief break to maybe whip something up. I do miss this place.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: SO MUCH EMOTION. WHAT TEARS. +1 :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Plenty of GRIMDARK HORROR on the site already, stuff like this that punches you in the feels needs more exploration. Take the +1.
